# services.nylon

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.nylon`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.nylon.<name>.acceptInterface` | `string` | Tell nylon which interface to listen for client requests on, default is "lo". |
| `services.nylon.<name>.allowedIPRanges` | `list of string` | Allowed client IP ranges are evaluated first, defaults to ARIN IPv4 private ranges: [ "192.168.0.0/16" "127.0.0.0/8" "172.16.0.0/12" "10.0.0.0/8" ] |
| `services.nylon.<name>.bindInterface` | `string` | Tell nylon which interface to use as an uplink, default is "enp3s0f0". |
| `services.nylon.<name>.deniedIPRanges` | `list of string` | Denied client IP ranges, these gets evaluated after the allowed IP ranges, defaults to all IPv4 addresses: [ "0.0.0.0/0" ] To block all other access than the allowed. |
| `services.nylon.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | Enables nylon as a running service upon activation. |
| `services.nylon.<name>.logging` | `boolean` | Enable logging, default is no logging. |
| `services.nylon.<name>.name` | `string` | The name of this nylon instance. |
| `services.nylon.<name>.nrConnections` | `signed integer` | The number of allowed simultaneous connections to the daemon, default 10. |
| `services.nylon.<name>.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | What port to listen for client requests, default is 1080. |
| `services.nylon.<name>.verbosity` | `boolean` | Enable verbose output, default is to not be verbose. |
